name: Sydney
summary: Sydney is Australia's biggest city, wrapped around a sparkling harbour with a giant arched bridge and a building shaped like billowing white sails. Beaches, ferries, and forest-filled parks are all within minutes of the city centre, and you might spot cockatoos, possums, or a humpback whale offshore.
funFacts: The Sydney Opera House looks like sails or seashells, and its roof is covered in more than one million shiny tiles made in Sweden.
Locals nicknamed the Sydney Harbour Bridge 'the Coathanger' because its huge steel arch curves like a hanger above the water.
Sydney sits on the land of the Gadigal people, who have lived around this harbour for tens of thousands of years before the city existed.
Wild sulphur-crested cockatoos, screechy white parrots with bright yellow mohawks, hang out in city parks and sometimes try to steal your sandwich.
Bondi Beach is so famous that lifeguards there star in their own TV show, watching swimmers between the red-and-yellow safety flags every day.
thingsToDo: Walk straight off the ship at Circular Quay to the Sydney Opera House and take a family tour beneath those giant white sail-shaped roofs.
Catch a yellow-and-green ferry across the harbour to Taronga Zoo and ride the Sky Safari cable car above koalas, kangaroos and Sumatran tigers.
Climb the BridgeClimb stairs or wander Cumberland Street to the Pylon Lookout for top-of-the-world views over the Sydney Harbour Bridge.
Wander the free Royal Botanic Garden next to the Opera House to spot cockatoos, ibises and a tiny stingless native bee hive near Maiden Theatre.
Explore The Rocks laneways behind the cruise terminal, where Sydney's oldest streets, weekend market stalls and meat-pie shops sit beside the harbour.
Hop a quick train or taxi to Bondi Beach for sandcastles, the kid-safe ocean pool and a stroll along the cliff path toward Tamarama.

localWord.meaning: Good day, in Gadigal, the language of Sydney's First People.

images.alt: The Sydney Opera House glowing white against a deep blue twilight sky, seen from the Harbour Bridge.
The mighty steel arch of the Sydney Harbour Bridge stretches over the water with the Opera House sails beside it.
Golden sand and turquoise waves curve along Sydney's famous Bondi Beach on a sunny day.
A fluffy grey koala clings to a eucalyptus branch at Taronga Zoo, looking sleepy and adorable.
A wide blue view of Sydney Harbour with sailboats and the city skyline, seen from high up at Taronga Zoo.
